title: Write-guard foreign-hold branch, net-first (frozen green before edit)
action: REGRESSION NET FIRST (critical pattern 20260716 — an over-denying guard can lock a session out of its own fix): freeze a byte-for-byte green net over the checkWrite decision table (guards.mjs:163-287 branches: direct-edit deny, docs/history code-ext deny, lane resolution, cross-session hold deny 204-228, terminal-phase intake, gated-phase, swarming reservation) BEFORE any edit, run it green against current code, keep it in skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_guards.mjs (post cs-2b split of the old test_lib.mjs monolith, this is where checkWrite/checkRead guard-lib tests now live). THEN: (1) new branch immediately after the existing cross-session hold branch — foreign-hold deny: when topology resolves (resolveRoots in try/catch; guards.mjs already imports from state.mjs for resolvePipeline), findForeignHolds(mainRoot, holder, [normalized]); deny names holding checkout + feature + expiry with the same voice as guards.mjs:216-222. Corrupt ledger => deny via holdsStoreCorrupt (reservationStoreCorrupt precedent 108-117); missing => open; topology unresolvable => fail-open (existing crash-log discipline). (2) DIRECT_EDIT_DENY (guards.mjs:73-76) gains .bee/runtime/cross-worktree-holds.json and .bee/runtime/worktree-grants.json (CLI-only writes). Tests: net stays green for every pre-existing row; new rows for foreign-hold deny (message names holder+expiry), own-hold allow, missing-ledger allow, corrupt-ledger deny, direct-edit deny for both runtime files. Self-onboard mirrors + plugin trees (hook adapters consume the vendored guards.mjs); full verify green.

truths: A frozen regression net over the full pre-change checkWrite decision table exists in test_lib.mjs and was proven green BEFORE the guard edit (evidence in trace).

checkWrite denies a write to a path ledger-held by a different checkout, naming holder + expiry; the acting checkout's own holds never deny; missing ledger allows; corrupt ledger denies with a typed kind; unresolvable topology fails open.
DIRECT_EDIT_DENY covers both .bee/runtime ledger files; hand Write/Edit of them is refused in every phase.
Every pre-existing guard behavior row is byte-identical (net green before and after).
Mirrors byte-identical (.bee/bin/lib/guards.mjs consumed by hooks); full run_verify.mjs green.
prohibitions: Do NOT reorder or alter existing checkWrite branches; the foreign-hold branch is strictly additive after branch 4.
Do NOT let any ledger/topology error path produce a deny except the corrupt-ledger case; fail-open discipline everywhere else.
Do NOT touch hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs dispatch logic — the change lives in guards.mjs.

trace.outcome: checkWrite gains an additive cross-worktree foreign-hold branch (net-first, frozen green before the edit); DIRECT_EDIT_DENY covers both .bee/runtime ledger files
deviations: A concurrent session's commit 87f5848 (docs(specs) [contention-split]) swept xwh-4's completed working-tree changes (guards.mjs + test_guards.mjs + all mirrors + onboarding.json + release-manifest refresh + xwh-4 claim bookkeeping) into its own commit before this worker could make the planned single 'feat(holds): ... [xwh-4]' commit — the xwh-4 implementation is therefore committed inside 87f5848 instead of a dedicated cell commit; only the cap bookkeeping gets its own [xwh-4] commit. One-commit-per-cell was violated by the other session's blanket commit, not by scope creep here.

Release-manifest refresh ran WITHOUT a stash-exclude: exec-cs4's WIP files were already committed (d61c143) and the working tree held no foreign modifications at refresh time, so there was nothing to exclude.
